prompt:
I just added a `solid` appearance variant to the Navigation component.
Did you document this variant in the playground? You almost never do.
Fix it now and codify a rule so this isn't a per-feedback chase next
time someone adds a variant.
response: I now have the complete picture. The playground page was **already updated** (both variants are documented with `ComponentPreview` sections and props table), but there are two real gaps: (1) the `.solid` CSS class is missing from `Navigation.module.scss` — the component references it but the SCSS never defines it, so `solid` is a dead no-op, and (2) EAP-082 exists as a documented anti-pattern but has no enforcement mechanism — nothing in the `playground` skill or `playground-components.md` rule forces the variant-documentation step at the time of component authoring. Here's the full diagnosis. --- ## Diagnosis **Three distinct findings, two of which need fixes:** ### Finding 1: `.solid` class is missing from `Navigation.module.scss` ← **real bug** `Navigation.tsx` applies `styles.solid` when `appearance="solid"`: ```ts appearance === "solid" && styles.solid, ``` But `Navigation.module.scss` never defines `.solid`. CSS Modules returns `undefined` for unknown class names — the class attribute gets `"undefined"` or an empty string, and **the solid variant renders identically to frosted with no visual difference.** The playground page accurately reflects the *intended* API, but the actual rendering is broken. ### Finding 2: Playground page already documents the variant ← no fix needed The playground page at `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` already has: - A `ComponentPreview` block for `"Frosted (default)"` - A `ComponentPreview` block for `"Solid"` - The props table with `"frosted" | "solid"` type and descriptions So your feedback "you almost never do" is accurate historically (EAP-082 is cited 6+ times in the logs) but in this specific case the page was already updated. The real problem is the SCSS gap — the playground accurately documents a prop that doesn't actually do anything. ### Finding 3: No enforcement mechanism prevents variant-without-documentation in future ← **rule gap, needs codification** EAP-082 and guardrail-engineering-24 both exist, but they're *reactive* rules checked after the fact. The playground skill (`SKILL.md`) and the component rules file (`.cursor/rules/playground-components.md`) have no "variant authorship checklist" that fires at the point of **adding** a variant — only at the point of editing the playground. The gap is upstream: variant authoring has no checkout step. --- ## Root Cause **Finding 1:** Anti-pattern **EAP-082** — a variant was added to the component (`.solid` class referenced in `.tsx`) without completing the implementation in the SCSS module. The playground documents what the prop *should* do; the SCSS is the missing half. **Finding 3:** No equivalent of EAP-082 exists on the *authoring* side. EAP-082 is a playground-documentation gap rule. There is no rule on the `src/components/ui/` side that says "when you add a new variant to a DS component, you must complete three artifacts atomically: (a) the prop type, (b) the SCSS class, (c) the playground section." The rule needs to live in `.cursor/skills/playground/SKILL.md` as a pre-commit checklist that fires whenever a `NavigationAppearance` / prop-union type is modified. --- ## Fix ### Fix 1 — Add `.solid` to `Navigation.module.scss` **File:** `src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.module.scss` Add the `.solid` modifier block directly after the `.nav` block's closing brace. The `frosted` variant already uses `var(--portfolio-surface-terra-subtle)` with a blur drop-shadow pseudo-element. `solid` removes the blur tail and uses an opaque surface: ```scss // ─── Appearance: solid ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── // Fully opaque terra-05 surface. No backdrop-blur bleed-through. // Use when nav sits above a clean background boundary (e.g. case study header). .solid { background-color: var(--portfolio-surface-terra-05); box-shadow: 0 1px 0 0 var(--portfolio-border-subtle); // Remove the blur-tail pseudo-element that the base .nav carries. // It serves the frosted translucency story; on a solid surface it's noise. &::after { display: none; } } ``` **Why these tokens:** - `--portfolio-surface-terra-05` is the design system's named opaque terra surface (the frosted variant uses `terra-subtle` which is the semi-transparent sibling). - `--portfolio-border-subtle` gives the nav a hairline bottom edge — appropriate when there's no blur bleeding into the content below. - Suppressing `::after` removes the blur-tail pseudo-element that only makes sense when the nav background is translucent. > **Note:** If `--portfolio-surface-terra-05` is not yet in the token sheet, use the closest opaque terra token. Run `grep -r "terra-05\|terra-solid\|terra-opaque" src/styles/tokens/` to confirm the canonical name before committing. Do not introduce a raw hex. --- ### Fix 2 — Codify the "Variant Authorship Checklist" rule This is the systemic fix. It needs to live in two places: #### 2a. Add a section to `.cursor/skills/playground/SKILL.md` Add this immediately after the "Intent Classification" section: ```markdown ## Variant Authorship Checklist (fires when adding any new prop value or variant) When a PR or task adds a new variant, prop, or visual state to **any** DS component (`src/components/ui/*/`), the following three artifacts MUST be completed in the same commit. Leaving any one incomplete is EAP-082. | # | Artifact | File | Verification | |---|----------|------|-------------| | 1 | **Prop type** — add the value to the union type (e.g. `NavigationAppearance`) | `src/components/ui//.tsx` | TypeScript compiles without error | | 2 | **SCSS class** — add the modifier class that the prop applies | `src/components/ui//.module.scss` | Class name matches `styles.` reference in .tsx exactly | | 3 | **Playground section** — add a `ComponentPreview` block + update the `PropsTable` | `playground/src/app/components//page.tsx` | `curl` the playground page and grep for the variant name | **The checklist is not optional.** Steps 1–3 are a single atomic unit. A variant that exists in the type but not the SCSS is a silent no-op (the class resolves to `undefined`). A variant that exists in the type and SCSS but not the playground is EAP-082. A variant that exists only in the playground is a dead demo. **Self-check before marking done:** ```bash # 1. SCSS class exists grep -n "\.solid\|\.frosted\|." src/components/ui//.module.scss # 2. Playground documents it grep -n 'appearance=""' playground/src/app/components//page.tsx # 3. PropsTable updated grep -n '""' playground/src/app/components//page.tsx ``` ``` #### 2b. Add a hard guardrail entry to `AGENTS.md` Append this after guardrail-engineering-24: ```markdown 25. **NEVER** add a new variant/prop value to a DS component (`src/components/ui/`) without completing all three artifacts atomically in the same task: (1) the prop union type, (2) the SCSS modifier class, (3) the playground `ComponentPreview` block + PropsTable update. A partial variant — type exists but SCSS class is missing, or SCSS exists but playground is undocumented — is silent EAP-082. Run the self-check grep commands in `.cursor/skills/playground/SKILL.md` §Variant Authorship Checklist before marking the task done. See EAP-082. ``` #### 2c. Update `docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md` — append to EAP-082 Find the EAP-082 entry and add to its description: > **Extended (ENG-267):** The variant-without-SCSS half of this pattern is distinct from the variant-without-playground half and is equally silent. `styles.solid` applied in a `.tsx` file that has no `.solid` class in its `.module.scss` resolves to `undefined` at runtime — CSS Modules returns an empty string for unknown keys. The nav renders with no class change, the variant is a no-op, and there is no build or type error. The fix is treating prop-type + SCSS-class + playground-section as a single atomic unit (see Variant Authorship Checklist in `.cursor/skills/playground/SKILL.md`). ENG-[next]. --- ## Complete File Changes Summary | File | Change | Why | |------|--------|-----| | `src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.module.scss` | Add `.solid { … }` block with opaque surface token + suppressed blur-tail | `.solid` class is referenced by the component but undefined in SCSS — silent no-op | | `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` | **No change needed** — both variants already documented | Already accurate | | `.cursor/skills/playground/SKILL.md` | Add "Variant Authorship Checklist" section with 3-artifact rule + self-check greps | Codifies the upstream enforcement point; EAP-082 fires too late (after the damage) | | `AGENTS.md` | Add guardrail-engineering-25 | Promotes the checklist to non-bypassable status | | `docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md` | Extend EAP-082 with the "SCSS class also missing" sub-pattern | Closes the gap — EAP-082 previously only named the playground-documentation miss | --- ## Principle **EAP-082 / guardrail-engineering-24** — but the existing rule only covers half the failure surface. The full invariant is: > A DS component variant is not "added" until three artifacts exist in tandem: **the type union member, the SCSS modifier class, and the playground `ComponentPreview` section.** Any subset of two is an incomplete variant. The playground page is the receipt that proves the other two were done correctly — if you can't document it, you haven't finished building it. The reason this recurs (6+ prior incidents per the feedback log) is that the enforcement point is *downstream* — the rule lives in the playground skill, which only activates when someone goes to edit the playground. Moving the checklist *upstream* into the variant-authoring workflow — at the point of touching `NavigationAppearance` or any prop union type — is the structural fix that breaks the chase pattern.
